sections.paragraph:
προστάτις, ιδος, ἡ (προΐστημι, cp. προστάτης; Cornutus 20 p. 37, 20; Lucian, Bis Accus. 29 θεὰ προστάτις ἑαυτῶν; Cass. Dio 42, 39 al.; PGM 36, 338; also pap ref. New Docs 4, 243) a woman in a supportive role, patron, benefactor (the relationship suggested by the term πρ. is not to be confused w. the Rom. patron-client system, which was of a different order and alien to Gk. tradition [s. JTouloumakos, Her 116, ’88, 304–24]) προστάτις πολλῶν ἐγενήθη καὶ ἐμοῦ αὐτοῦ she has proved to be of great assistance to many, including myself Ro 16:2 (Ltzm., Hdb. ad loc. The masc. προστάτης took on a technical sense and is found w. this mng. in Israelite [Schürer III, 102] as well as in polyth. [OGI 209; SIG 1109, 13; CIG I, 126; GHeinrici, ZWT 19, 1876, 516ff.—EZiebarth, Das griech. Vereinswesen 1896, index s.v.; Poland, Gesch., index s.v.; WOtto, Priester u. Tempel im hellenist. Ägypten II 1908 p. 75, 1] religious circles).—S. preceding entry. On women as benefactors, s. RvanBremen, in Images of Women in Antiquity, ed. ACameron/AKuhrt ’83, 223–42; COsiek, Women in the Ancient Mediterranean World: BR 39, ’94, 57–61 (NT). New Docs 4, 242–44. M-M.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Cornutus Cornutus , I a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

ref. ref. = reference(s)

Gk. Gk. = Greek

Her Her = Hermes, Zeitschrift für klassische Philologie—List 6

Ltzm. Ltzm. = HLietzmann, commentator and editor of various works

Hdb. Hdb. = Handbuch (in connection with a specific NT book, the ref. is to a commentary in the series Handbuch zum Neuen Testament, founded by HLietzmann)—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

masc. masc. = masculine

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

polyth. polyth. = polytheist, polytheism, polytheistic

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

CIG CIG = Corpus Inscriptionum Graecarum—List 3

ZWT ZWT = Zeitschrift für Wissenschaftliche Theologie—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Poland Poland = FP., Geschichte des griechischen Vereinswesen—Lists 6

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

BR BR = Biblical Research—List 6

NT NT = New Testament

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 885
